chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Production Systems Engineering. (3-0). Credit 3. Principles, models, and techniques for planning and analysis of production and distribution systems; application of linear, integer, and nonlinear optimization models and solution methods for aggregate planning, supply chain planning, push (MRP) and pull (JIT) material flow management, inventory control under deterministic and stochastic demands, operations scheduling, and production scheduling. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ISEN 230 and ISEN 320.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in ISEN 230 and ISEN 320.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
• describe different types and levels of decision making in a production system
• model, formulate, solve, analyze problems arising in aggregate production and supply chain planning
• model and analyze fundamental inventory control systems with certain/uncertain demand
• describe and analyze push (MRP) and pull production systems (JIT)
• model and solve deterministic operations scheduling problems
• model and solve basic project scheduling problems

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Assignments (Home Works or other): 10%
Exams: 90%
There will be four exams:
• The first three exams are not comprehensive and will cover roughly one third of course materials.
• At the end of the course, one optional comprehensive final exam will be held.
• The lowest exam grade out of these four exams will be dropped, and the overall course grade will be based on the four remaining exam grades at 30% weight each, towards the overall course grade.
Final grades will be assigned as follows:
A: [90,100] B: [80-90) C: [70,80) D: [50,70) F: [0,50)

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
Topical Outline and Exams Schedule
• Introduction to Production and Operations Strategy
• Forecasting
• Aggregate Production Planning
• Inventory Control: Known Demand
• Inventory Control: Uncertain Demand
• Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and Just-in-Time (JIT)
• Operations Scheduling
• Project Scheduling (time permitting)

chunks.title: University Policies
chunks.content: This section outlines the university-level policies that must be included in each course syllabus. The TAMU Faculty Advisory Council established the wording of these policies.

chunks.title: Statement on Mental Health and Wellness
chunks.content:
Texas A&M University recognizes that mental health and wellness are critical factors influencing a student's academic success and overall wellbeing. Students are encouraged to engage in healthy self-care practices by utilizing the resources and services available through University Health Services. The TELUS Health Student Support app provides access to professional counseling in multiple languages anytime, anywhere by phone or chat, and the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline offers 24-hour emergency support at 988 or 988lifeline.org.
Texas A&M College Station
Students needing a listening ear can contact University Health Services at 979.458.4584. Call 911 or visit your nearest emergency room if you are currently experiencing a life-threatening situation or if your safety is at risk. 24-hour emergency help is also available through the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (988) or at 988lifeline.org.
